title: euonymus schottii
intro: Euonymus schottii, also known as the Schott's spindle tree or Western wahoo, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Euonymus schottii include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
